Title: IDocumentResolver
Description: A factory which can resolve a [`JsonElement`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.text.json.jsonelement) from a [`JsonReference`](/api/v4/corvus-json-jsonreference.html).
Body:
A factory which can resolve a [`JsonElement`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.text.json.jsonelement) from a [`JsonReference`](/api/v4/corvus-json-jsonreference.html).
It is disposable so that it can manage the lifetime of the cached documents.
TryResolve: Gets the element from the document at the given [`Uri`](/api/v4/corvus-json-jsonreference.uri.html) in the `reference`.
AddDocument: Add an existing document to the cache.
Reset: Reset the document resolver.

Body: Add an existing document to the cache.

Title: Period
Description: Represents a period of time expressed in human chronological terms: hours, days, weeks, months and so on.
Body:
Represents a period of time expressed in human chronological terms: hours, days, weeks, months and so on.
A [`Period`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.html) contains a set of properties such as [`Years`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.years.html), [`Months`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.months.html), and so on that return the number of each unit contained within this period. Note that these properties are not normalized in any way by default, and so a [`Period`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.html) may contain values such as "2 hours and 90 minutes". The [`Normalize`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.normalize.html) method will convert equivalent periods into a standard representation. Periods can contain negative units as well as positive units ("+2 hours, -43 minutes, +10 seconds"), but do not differentiate between properties that are zero and those that are absent (i.e. a period created as "10 years" and one created as "10 years, zero months" are equal periods; the [`Months`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.months.html) property returns zero in both cases). [`Period`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.html) equality is implemented by comparing each property's values individually, without any normalization. (For example, a period of "24 hours" is not considered equal to a period of "1 day".) The static [`NormalizingEqualityComparer`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.normalizingequalitycomparer.html) comparer provides an equality comparer which performs normalization before comparisons. There is no natural ordering for periods, but [`CreateComparer`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.createcomparer.html) can be used to create a comparer which orders periods according to a reference date, by adding each period to that date and comparing the results. Periods operate on calendar-related types such as [`LocalDateTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.LocalDateTime.html) whereas [`Duration`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.Duration.html) operates on instants on the time line. (Note that although [`ZonedDateTime`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.ZonedDateTime.html) includes both concepts, it only supports duration-based arithmetic.) The complexity of each method in this type is hard to document precisely, and often depends on the calendar system involved in performing the actual calculations. Operations do not depend on the magnitude of the units in the period, other than for optimizations for values of zero or occasionally for particularly small values. For example, adding 10,000 days to a date does not require greater algorithmic complexity than adding 1,000 days to the same date.
Zero: Gets a period containing only zero-valued properties.
NormalizingEqualityComparer: Gets an equality comparer which compares periods by first normalizing them - so 24 hours is deemed equal to 1 day, and so on. Note that as per the [`Normalize`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.normalize.html) method, years and months are unchanged by normalization - so 12 months does not equal 1 year.
Nanoseconds: Gets the number of nanoseconds within this period.
Ticks: Gets the number of ticks within this period.
Milliseconds: Gets the number of milliseconds within this period.
Seconds: Gets the number of seconds within this period.
Minutes: Gets the number of minutes within this period.
Hours: Gets the number of hours within this period.
Days: Gets the number of days within this period.
Weeks: Gets the number of weeks within this period.
Months: Gets the number of months within this period.
Years: Gets the number of years within this period.
HasTimeComponent: Gets a value indicating whether or not this period contains any non-zero-valued time-based properties (hours or lower).
HasDateComponent: Gets a value indicating whether or not this period contains any non-zero date-based properties (days or higher).
TryParse: Parses a string into a Period.
Parse: Parses a string into a Period.
Parse: Parses a string into a Period.
FromYears: Creates a period representing the specified number of years.
FromMonths: Creates a period representing the specified number of months.
FromWeeks: Creates a period representing the specified number of weeks.
FromDays: Creates a period representing the specified number of days.
FromHours: Creates a period representing the specified number of hours.
FromMinutes: Creates a period representing the specified number of minutes.
FromSeconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of seconds.
FromMilliseconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of milliseconds.
FromTicks: Creates a period representing the specified number of ticks.
FromNanoseconds: Creates a period representing the specified number of nanoseconds.
Add: Adds two periods together, by simply adding the values for each property.
CreateComparer: Creates an [`IComparer`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.collections.generic.icomparer-1) for periods, using the given "base" local date/time.
Subtract: Subtracts one period from another, by simply subtracting each property value.
DaysBetween: Returns the number of days between two [`LocalDate`](https://www.nodatime.org/3.3.x/api/NodaTime.LocalDate.html) objects.
PeriodParser: A parser for a json period.
ToDuration: For periods that do not contain a non-zero number of years or months, returns a duration for this period assuming a standard 7-day week, 24-hour day, 60-minute hour etc.
Normalize: Returns a normalized version of this period, such that equivalent (but potentially non-equal) periods are changed to the same representation.
ToString: Returns this string formatted according to the ISO8601 duration specification used by JSON schema.
Equals: Compares the given object for equality with this one, as per [`Equals`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.equals.html). See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
GetHashCode: Returns the hash code for this period, consistent with [`Equals`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.equals.html). See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
Equals: Compares the given period for equality with this one. See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.
Equals: Compares the given period for equality with this one. See the type documentation for a description of equality semantics.

Title: PeriodBuilder
Description: A mutable builder class for [`Period`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.html) values. Each property can be set independently, and then a Period can be created from the result using the [`BuildPeriod`](/api/v4/corvus-json-periodbuilder.buildperiod.html) method.
Body:
A mutable builder class for [`Period`](/api/v4/corvus-json-period.html) values. Each property can be set independently, and then a Period can be created from the result using the [`BuildPeriod`](/api/v4/corvus-json-periodbuilder.buildperiod.html) method.
Years: Gets or sets the number of years within the period.
Months: Gets or sets the number of months within the period.
Weeks: Gets or sets the number of weeks within the period.
Days: Gets or sets the number of days within the period.
Hours: Gets or sets the number of hours within the period.
Minutes: Gets or sets the number of minutes within the period.
Seconds: Gets or sets the number of seconds within the period.
Milliseconds: Gets or sets the number of milliseconds within the period.
Ticks: Gets or sets the number of ticks within the period.
Nanoseconds: Gets or sets the number of nanoseconds within the period.
this[PeriodUnits]: Gets or sets the value of a single unit.
BuildPeriod: Builds a period from the properties in this builder.

Title: ValidationContext
Description: The current validation context.
Body:
The current validation context.
IsValid: Gets a value indicating whether the context is valid.
IsUsingResults: Gets a value indicating whether this is using results.
IsUsingStack: Gets a value indicating whether this is using the location stack.
IsUsingEvaluatedProperties: Gets a value indicating whether this is using evaluated properties.
IsUsingEvaluatedItems: Gets a value indicating whether this is using evaluated items.
Results: Gets the validation results.
UsingResults: Use the results set.
UsingStack: Use the keyword stack.
ForceUsingStack: Force using the keyword stack.
UsingEvaluatedProperties: Use the evaluated properties set.
UsingEvaluatedItems: Use the evaluated properties set.
HasEvaluatedLocalProperty: Determines if a property has been locally evaluated.
HasEvaluatedLocalItemIndex: Determines if an item has been locally evaluated.
HasEvaluatedLocalOrAppliedProperty: Determines if a property has been evaluated locally or by applied schema.
HasEvaluatedLocalOrAppliedItemIndex: Determines if an item has been evaluated locally or by applied schema.
WithResult: Adds a result to the validation context.
WithResult: Adds a result to the validation context.
WithLocalItemIndex: Adds an item index to the evaluated items array.
WithLocalProperty: Adds a property name to the evaluated properties array.
MergeChildContext: Merges the local and applied evaluated entities from a child context into the applied evaluated entities in a parent context.
PushSchemaLocation: Pushes a location onto the location stack for the context.
PushDocumentProperty: Pushes a location onto the location stack for the context.
PushDocumentArrayIndex: Pushes a location onto the location stack for the context.
PushValidationLocationProperty: Pushes a location onto the location stack for the context.
PushValidationLocationReducedPathModifier: Pushes the reduced path modifier onto the location stack for the context.
ReplaceValidationLocationReducedPathModifier: Replaces the reduced path modifier on the location stack for the context.
PushValidationLocationReducedPathModifierAndProperty: Pushes the reduced path modifier and property name onto the location and document stacks for the context.
PushValidationLocationArrayIndex: Pushes a location onto the location stack for the context.
PopLocation: Pops a location off the location stack.
CreateChildContext: Creates a child context from the current location.
MergeResults: Merge the messages for a given set of results into this result, applying the given validity at the end, regardless of the individual validity of the results in the set.

Body:
A [`StringBuilder`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.text.stringbuilder)0like class that supports code generation.
Initializes a new instance of the [`CodeGenerator`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-codegenerator.html) class.

EndFile: End creating a new file.
EnsureCapacity: Ensures that the capacity of this builder is at least the specified value.
EndsWith: Gets a value indicating whether the builder ends with the given character sequence.
ToString: Creates a string from a substring of this builder.

PushIndent: Increase the indent.
PopIndent: Decrease the indent.
PushMemberScope: Enter a new member name scope.
PopMemberScope: Leave a member name scope.
GetChunks: GetChunks returns ChunkEnumerator that follows the IEnumerable pattern and thus can be used in a C# 'foreach' statements to retrieve the data in the StringBuilder as chunks (ReadOnlyMemory) of characters. An example use is: ```csharp foreach (ReadOnlyMemory<char> chunk in sb.GetChunks()) foreach (char c in chunk.Span) { /* operation on c } ``` It is undefined what happens if the StringBuilder is modified while the chunk enumeration is incomplete. StringBuilder is also not thread-safe, so operating on it with concurrent threads is illegal. Finally the ReadOnlyMemory chunks returned are NOT guaranteed to remain unchanged if the StringBuilder is modified, so do not cache them for later use either. This API's purpose is efficiently extracting the data of a CONSTANT StringBuilder. Creating a ReadOnlySpan from a ReadOnlyMemory (the .Span property) is expensive compared to the fetching of the character, so create a local variable for the SPAN if you need to use it in a nested for statement. For example: ```csharp foreach (ReadOnlyMemory<char> chunk in sb.GetChunks()) { var span = chunk.Span; for (int i = 0; i < span.Length; i++) { /* operation on span[i] */ } } ```

Title: CodeGenerator.GetChunks
Description: GetChunks returns ChunkEnumerator that follows the IEnumerable pattern and thus can be used in a C# 'foreach' statements to retrieve the data in the StringBuilder as chunks (ReadOnlyMemory) of characters. An example use is: ```csharp foreach (ReadOnlyMemory<char> chunk in sb.GetChunks()) foreach (char c in chunk.Span) { /* operation on c } ``` It is undefined what happens if the StringBuilder is modified while the chunk enumeration is incomplete. StringBuilder is also not thread-safe, so operating on it with concurrent threads is illegal. Finally the ReadOnlyMemory chunks returned are NOT guaranteed to remain unchanged if the StringBuilder is modified, so do not cache them for later use either. This API's purpose is efficiently extracting the data of a CONSTANT StringBuilder. Creating a ReadOnlySpan from a ReadOnlyMemory (the .Span property) is expensive compared to the fetching of the character, so create a local variable for the SPAN if you need to use it in a nested for statement. For example: ```csharp foreach (ReadOnlyMemory<char> chunk in sb.GetChunks()) { var span = chunk.Span; for (int i = 0; i < span.Length; i++) { /* operation on span[i] */ } } ```

Title: IValidationHandler
Description: Handles validation for a particular type of validator.
Body:
Handles validation for a particular type of validator.
Handlers for specific keywords implement [`IKeywordValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ikeywordvalidationhandler.html) and are registered with a [`KeywordValidationHandlerRegistry`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-keywordvalidationhandlerregistry.html) which a [`ILanguageProvider`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ilanguageprovider.html) instance uses to generate validation code for a [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html). While most [`ILanguageProvider`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ilanguageprovider.html) implementations will register a standard set of [`IValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ivalidationhandler.html) instances, using custom vocabularies may require you to add new [`IKeywordValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ikeywordvalidationhandler.html) or child [`IValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ivalidationhandler.html) instances to add custom validation capabilities.
ValidationHandlerPriority: Gets the relative priority for the [`IValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ivalidationhandler.html).

Body: Gets the relative priority for the [`IValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ivalidationhandler.html).

Title: TypeDeclaration
Description: A type declaration built from a JSON schema, by calling [`AddTypeDeclarationsAsync`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarationsasync.html) or [`AddTypeDeclarations`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarations.html).
Body:
A type declaration built from a JSON schema, by calling [`AddTypeDeclarationsAsync`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarationsasync.html) or [`AddTypeDeclarations`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarations.html).
The type declaration build goes through two phases. First it is constructed from a [`LocatedSchema`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.locatedschema.html), obtained from the [`JsonSchemaRegistry`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschemaregistry.html). Subschema are discovered from any [`ISubschemaTypeBuilderKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-isubschematypebuilderkeyword.html) in the [`Vocabulary`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-locatedschema.vocabulary.html), and similarly, any [`IReferenceKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ireferencekeyword.html) are resolved. This will involve loading any newly discovered base schema into the [`JsonSchemaRegistry`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschemaregistry.html). Then, its internal [`BuildComplete`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.buildcomplete.html) flag is set to `true` - all schema and subschema are fully resolved and the [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html) can now be used for any further analysis.
SubschemaTypeDeclarations: Gets the subschema type declarations.
OrderedSubschemaTypeDeclarations: Gets the subschema type declarations, ordered by location.
LocatedSchema: Gets the located schema for the type declaration.
RelativeSchemaLocation: Gets the schema location relative to the root schema.
PropertyDeclarations: Gets the property declarations for this type declaration.
HasPropertyDeclarations: Gets a value indicating whether the type has any property declarations.
BuildComplete: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the basic build process is complete.
UpdateLocation: Update the location.
AddSubschemaTypeDeclaration: Adds a type declaration for a subschema to this type declaration.
SetMetadata: Sets a metadata value.
RemoveMetadata: Sets a metadata value.
TryGetMetadata: Gets a metadata value set for the type declaration.
AddOrUpdatePropertyDeclaration: Add or update a [`PropertyDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-propertydeclaration.html).
HasKeyword: Gets a value indicating if the keyword is present on the type declaration.
TryGetKeyword: Tries to get the value of the keyword.

Body: A type declaration built from a JSON schema, by calling [`AddTypeDeclarationsAsync`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarationsasync.html) or [`AddTypeDeclarations`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-jsonschematypebuilder.addtypedeclarations.html).

Title: TypeDeclarationExtensions
Description: Extension methods for [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html).
Body:
Extension methods for [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html).
HasSiblingHidingKeyword: Gets a value indicating whether this type declaration has a sibling-hiding keyword.
ExplicitContentMediaType: Gets the explicit content media type for the type declaration.
ExplicitContentEncoding: Gets the explicit content media type for the type declaration.
IsDeprecated: Gets a value indicating whether the type is deprecated.
HasExclusiveMaximumModifier: Gets a value indicating whether the type has an exclusive maximum modifier.
HasExclusiveMinimumModifier: Gets a value indicating whether the type has an exclusive minimum modifier.
RequiresStringValueValidation: Gets a value indicating whether the type requires string value validation.
RequiresStringLength: Gets a value indicating whether the type requires the string length in string validation.
RequiresPropertyCount: Gets a value indicating whether the type requires the property count in object validation.
PatternProperties: Gets the pattern property declarations for the type declaration.
ReducedTypeDeclaration: Gets the reduced type declaration for the type.
TryGetDynamicSource: Try to get the dynamic source for a type declaration.
IfSubschemaType: Gets the 'if' subschema type, if available.
ThenSubschemaType: Gets the 'then' subschema type, if available.
PropertyNamesSubschemaType: Gets the 'property names' subschema type, if available.
ElseSubschemaType: Gets the 'else' subschema type, if available.
DependentRequired: Gets the dependent required declarations for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
DependentSchemasSubschemaTypes: Gets the dependent schema declarations for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
AllOfCompositionTypes: Gets the AllOf composition types for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
AnyOfCompositionTypes: Gets the AnyOf composition types for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
AnyOfConstantValues: Gets the AnyOf constant values for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
OneOfCompositionTypes: Gets the OneOf composition types for the type declaration, grouped by the providing keyword.
SingleConstantValue: Gets the single constant value on the type.
ExplicitSingleConstantValue: Gets the single constant value on the type.
ExplicitFormat: Gets the format that is explicitly defined on the type.
IsFormatAssertion: Gets a value indicating whether the format is an assertion.
Format: Gets the format for the type.
ArrayRank: Gets the rank for the array, or null if the item is not an array.
IsMapObject: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration is a map object.
IsTuple: Gets a value indicating whether this is a "pure" tuple (i.e. it only permits a fixed set of array items and no others).
IsFixedSizeNumericArray: Gets a value indicating whether this is a fixed-size numeric array.
IsFixedSizeArray: Gets a value indicating whether this is a fixed size array in all ranks.
IsNumericArray: Gets a value indicating whether this is a numeric array in all ranks.
ArrayValueBufferSize: Gets the value buffer size for the full fixed-size array in all ranks.
ArrayDimension: Gets the dimension for the array, or null if the item is not an array.
ExplicitArrayItemsType: Gets the type of a strongly-typed array, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed array.
ArrayItemsType: Gets the type of a strongly-typed array, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed array.
FallbackObjectPropertyType: Gets the type of properties in a strongly-typed object, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed object.
ExplicitRequiredProperties: Gets the collection of explicitly required properties in an object, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed object.
ExplicitProperties: Gets the collection of explicitly required properties in an object, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed object.
LocalEvaluatedPropertyType: Gets the type of additional properties (evaluated in the local scope) in a strongly-typed object, or `null` if no such type is defined.
LocalAndAppliedEvaluatedPropertyType: Gets the type of additional properties (evaluated in the local-and-applied scope) in a strongly-typed object, or `null` if no such type is defined.
ExplicitNonTupleItemsType: Gets the type of non-tuple types in a typed array, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed array.
NonTupleItemsType: Gets the type of a strongly-typed array, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed array.
ExplicitUnevaluatedItemsType: Gets the type of unevaluated item types in a typed array, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed array.
ExplicitTupleType: Gets the explicit type of a tuple, or `null` if the type is not explicitly a strongly-typed tuple.
ImplicitTupleType: Gets the implicit type of a tuple, or `null` if the type is not explicitly a strongly-typed tuple.
TupleType: Gets the type of a tuple, or `null` if the type is not a strongly-typed tuple.
IsInDefinitionsContainer: Gets a value indicating whether this type declaration is defined in a definitions container.
RequiresItemsEvaluationTracking: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires items evaluation tracking.
RequiresArrayLength: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires array length tracking.
RequiresArrayEnumeration: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires array enumeration.
RequiresObjectEnumeration: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires object enumeration.
RequiresPropertyEvaluationTracking: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires property evaluation tracking.
RequiresJsonValueKind: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration requires the value kind.
HasDefaultValue: Indicates whether the type declaration has a default value.
DefaultValue: Gets the default value for an instance.
CompositionTypeDeclarations: Gets the type declarations provided by [`ICompositionKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-icompositionkeyword.html) keywords for the type declaration.
Keywords: Gets the [`IKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ikeyword.html) keywords for the type declaration.
ValidationKeywords: Gets the [`IValidationKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-ivalidationkeyword.html) keywords for the type declaration.
DefinitionsKeywords: Gets the [`IDefinitionsKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-keywords-idefinitionskeyword.html) keywords for the type declaration.
TypeNameProviderKeywords: Gets the [`ITypeNameProviderKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-itypenameproviderkeyword.html) keywords for the type declaration.
OrderedValidationHandlers: Gets the ordered validation handlers of a specific type for the type declaration.
OrderedValidationHandlers: Gets the ordered validation handlers for the type declaration.
CanReduce: Gets a value indicating whether the type declaration can be reduced.
ImpliedCoreTypesOrAny: Gets a value indicating the core types represented by the type declaration.
ImpliedCoreTypes: Gets a value indicating the core types represented by the type declaration.
LocallyImpliedCoreTypes: Gets a value indicating the core types represented by the type declaration, considering only locally implied types, not composite subschema.
AllowedCoreTypes: Gets the union of [`CoreTypes`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-coretypes.html) to match for type validation, or [`None`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-coretypes.none.html) if any type is valid.
ShortDocumentation: Gets the (optional) short documentation for this type declaration.
LongDocumentation: Gets the (optional) long documentation for this type declaration.
Examples: Gets the (optional) examples for this type declaration.
ValidationConstants: Gets the (optional) validation constants for this type declaration.
TypedValidationConstants: Gets the (optional) typed validation constants for this type declaration.
ValidationRegularExpressions: Gets the (optional) validation regular expressions for this type declaration.

Title: DefaultNameCollisionResolver
Description: The default name collision resolver.
Body:
The default name collision resolver.
This resolver always succeeds, so it is set at the lowest possible priority. It is intended to be used as a fallback when no other resolvers can resolve a name collision. If you have specialized requirements, you can add resolvers at a higher priority.

Title: StringValidationHandler
Description: A validation handler for [`IStringValidationKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-istringvalidationkeyword.html) capability.
Body:
A validation handler for [`IStringValidationKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-istringvalidationkeyword.html) capability.
The child handler code is emitted in a context where the following values are in scope. ```csharp ReadOnlySpan<char> input; int length; in ValidationContextWrapper context; out ValidationContext result; ``` The result should be updated with the result of validation. Length is available only if a [`IStringValidationKeyword`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-istringvalidationkeyword.html) present on the type declaration asserted [`RequiresStringLength`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-istringvalidationkeyword.requiresstringlength.html) to be `true`.
Instance: Gets a singleton instance of the [`StringValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-csharp-stringvalidationhandler.html).

Title: StringValidationHandler.Instance
Description: Gets a singleton instance of the [`StringValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-csharp-stringvalidationhandler.html).
Body: Gets a singleton instance of the [`StringValidationHandler`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-csharp-stringvalidationhandler.html).

Title: TypeDeclarationExtensions
Description: Extension methods for [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html).
Body:
Extension methods for [`TypeDeclaration`](/api/v4/corvus-json-codegeneration-typedeclaration.html).
IsCorvusJsonExtendedType: Gets a value indicating whether this is a Corvus extended JSON type.
TryGetCorvusJsonExtendedTypeName: Try to get the Corvus extended type name for the given type declaration.
PreferredBinaryJsonNumberKind: Gets a value indicating whether this type prefers 128bit integers.
AlwaysAssertFormat: Gets a value indicating whether to always assert format, regardless of the vocabulary.
FindChildNameCollision: Determines if there is a child of the parent type declaration whose name matches the proposed name for a given child type declaration.
OptionalAsNullable: Gets a value indicating whether to generate optional properties as nullable.
UseImplicitOperatorString: Gets a value indicating whether to generate an implicit operator for conversion to `string`.
AddExplicitUsings: Gets a value indicating whether to generate using statements for the standard implicit usings.
CollidesWithParent: Determines if the given name collides with the parent name.
MatchesExistingTypeInParent: Determines if the given name collides with another child in the parent.
IsBuiltInJsonAnyType: Gets a value which determines if this type is the built-in JsonAny type.
IsCorvusJsonExtendedJsonAny: Gets a value which determines if this type is the built-in JsonAny type.
IsCorvusJsonExtendedJsonNotAny: Gets a value which determines if this type is the built-in JsonAny type.
MatchesExistingPropertyNameInParent: Determines if the given name collides with a property name in the parent.
PreferredDotnetNumericTypeName: Gets the preferred .NET numeric type (e.g. int, double, long etc) for the type declaration.
PreferredDotnetStringFormatTypeName: Gets the preferred .NET string format type (e.g. JsonUuid, JsonIri etc) for the type declaration.
FullyQualifiedDotnetTypeName: Gets the fully qualified .NET type name.
DotnetTypeNameWithoutNamespace: Gets the .NET type name fully qualified, but without the namespace.
DotnetNamespace: Gets the .NET namespace.
Parent: Gets the .NET namespace.
SetParent: Sets the parent type declaration for a child.
Children: Gets the children of a type declaration.
DoNotGenerate: Gets a value indicating that this type declaration should not be generated.
SetDoNotGenerate: Sets a value indicating that this type declaration should not be generated.
DotnetTypeName: Gets the .NET type name.
HasDotnetTypeName: Gets a value indicating whether the .NET type name has been set for the type declaration.
TryGetDotnetTypeName: Tries to get the .NET type name for the type declaration.
SetDotnetNamespace: Sets the .NET namespace.
SetDotnetAccessibility: Sets the .NET accessibility.
DotnetAccessibility: Gets the .NET accessibility.
SetDotnetTypeName: Sets the .NET type name.

Title: UriTemplate
Description: Implements a URI template conforming to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570, built over [`JsonAny`](/api/v4/corvus-json-jsonany.html).
Body:
Implements a URI template conforming to http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570, built over [`JsonAny`](/api/v4/corvus-json-jsonany.html).
This is modelled on the Tavis.UriTemplate API, and offers mechanisms for parsing parameters from a URI according to a URI template and also resolving a template to a URI based on a provided set of parameters. Note that this is not a low-allocation type. In particular, processing a URI template to create the template extraction parser is an expensive operation. If you do not need this functionality for a particular instance, you should ensure that you set the `createParameterParser` constructor parameter to `false`. For low-allocation scenarios you should use the low-level `UriTemplateParserFactory` to create and cache an `IUriTemplateParser` instance if you are doing parameter extraction. or use a `UriTemplateResolver` if you are resolving a template from parameters.
